questions.question: Which statement best defines First-order elimination?
questions.option_b: Dose to achieve target Cp quickly
questions.option_c: Nonlinear kinetics with Km and Vmax
questions.explanation: Most drugs follow this; half-life is constant.

questions.question: Which statement best defines Zero-order elimination?
questions.option_d: Dose to achieve target Cp quickly
questions.explanation: Occurs when pathways saturated (e.g., ethanol).

questions.question: Which statement best defines Clearance (CL)?
questions.option_c: Determines maintenance dose rate at steady state.
questions.explanation: Determines maintenance dose rate at steady state.

questions.question: Which statement best defines Volume of distribution (Vd)?
questions.explanation: Large Vd suggests tissue distribution.

questions.question: Which statement best defines Half-life (t1/2)?
questions.explanation: t1/2 = 0.693·Vd/CL (first-order).

questions.question: Which statement best defines Bioavailability (F)?
questions.option_d: Concentration-time not proportional to dose at high concentrations.
questions.explanation: Impacts oral dose vs IV dose.

questions.question: Which statement best defines AUC?
questions.explanation: AUC ∝ Dose/CL for linear kinetics.

questions.question: Which statement best defines Steady state?
questions.explanation: Reached after ~4–5 half-lives.

questions.question: Which statement best defines Loading dose?
questions.option_a: Most drugs follow this; half-life is constant.
questions.option_d: Dose to achieve target Cp quickly
questions.explanation: LD = Vd·Cp_target/F.

questions.question: Which statement best defines Michaelis–Menten kinetics?
questions.option_b: Nonlinear kinetics with Km and Vmax
questions.option_c: Most drugs follow this; half-life is constant.
questions.option_d: Dose to achieve target Cp quickly
questions.explanation: Concentration-time not proportional to dose at high concentrations.
